title: Cheirinia × helvetica
taxonomy: Cheirinia × helvetica is a hybrid species of flowering plant, classified as a member of the family Asteraceae. It is a part of the Cheirinia genus, which is itself part of the larger group of dandelion-like plants. The classification of Cheirinia × helvetica is further supported by its morphological characteristics, including its leaf shape and flower structure. As a hybrid, its classification is distinct from its parent species and is often noted in botanical nomenclature. The classification of Cheirinia × helvetica is subject to ongoing research and refinement in the field of botany.

intro: Cheirinia × helvetica is a hybrid plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing and the treatment of infections. The bioactive constituents of Cheirinia × helvetica include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
